summary.what_happened: Report generation latency rose from about 10:30 and saturated above 2 seconds; the incident is ongoing.
summary.customer_impact: Report pages load slowly or time out.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-10T11:05:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; deviated series not recovered -> ongoing

severity.explanation: reports-service p95 reached 2400ms against a 300ms baseline, 8x, inside the >4x-latency SEV-2 band; no error-rate series is present to justify SEV-1

timeline.description: io_wait_ms first deviated >=2.0x from baseline (5.0 ms -> 18.0 ms)
WARN: disk I/O latency above threshold on /dev/nvme1: 18ms avg over 60s
p95_latency_ms first deviated >=2.0x from baseline (300.0 ms -> 780.0 ms)
ERROR: slow query: SELECT report_rows aggregation took 2100ms (threshold 500ms)
ERROR: slow query: SELECT report_rows aggregation took 3900ms (threshold 500ms)
config 2026.06.10-1058 on reports-service: Update dashboard footer copyright text (static template only, no code paths changed)
Alert fired: High p95 latency on reports-service GET /reports
ERROR: report generation timed out after 2000ms waiting for reports-db

evidence.interpretation: deploy 2026.06.10-1058 (footer copy, config-only) landed 10:58 - 28 minutes AFTER the io_wait deviation began at 10:30; timing rules it out as the trigger
reports-db disk I/O latency warning at 10:31 precedes all application symptoms
slow aggregation queries escalate 2100ms -> 3900ms between 10:41 and 10:52
reports-service p95 first crossed 2x baseline at 10:40 (780ms)
reports-db io_wait first deviated at 10:30 (18ms vs 5ms baseline) - BEFORE any change in the window

hypotheses.title: reports-db disk I/O degradation slowing aggregation queries
hypotheses.statement: reports-db io_wait rose from 10:30, slow queries followed, and reports-service latency saturated waiting on the database - consistent with storage-level degradation on reports-db.
assumptions: package telemetry is complete for the window
recommended_checks: check reports-db host SMART/disk metrics and cloud volume events
compare query plans against last week's for the same aggregation

hypotheses.title: Deploy 2026.06.10-1058 caused the latency increase
hypotheses.statement: A deploy landed inside the window, but symptoms began 28 minutes before it and the change is a static footer text update - the timing and content both argue against it.
assumptions: package telemetry is complete for the window
recommended_checks: confirm the deploy diff touches no query or pool code

missing_data.impact: cannot identify slow spans or failing dependencies
no operational guidance or known failure modes available

confirm the deploy diff touches no query or pool code

signals.recovered_when: within 10% of baseline 5 ms for >= 3 consecutive points
within 10% of baseline 300 ms for >= 3 consecutive points

log_patterns_should_stop: slow query: SELECT report_rows aggregation took Nms (threshold Nms)
recovery_verification.re_alert_condition: p95_latency_ms on reports-service crosses the alert threshold 900 again

checks.detail: SEV-2 is within the numeric ceiling (SEV-2)

communication_drafts.internal_update: SEV-2, ongoing: report generation degraded. Leading hypothesis (medium confidence): disk I/O degradation on reports-db - its io_wait rose 28 minutes before the only deploy in the window, which is a config-only footer change ranked low. No remediation has been executed; options await human approval.

postmortem_draft.title: Postmortem draft: reports latency 2026-06-10
postmortem_draft.summary: reports-db storage latency degraded from 10:30; aggregation queries slowed and report generation saturated above 2s.
postmortem_draft.impact: Report pages degraded ~50 minutes and ongoing.
contributing_factors: likely: storage-level I/O degradation on reports-db
ruled unlikely: the 10:58 config-only deploy (postdates onset)
open_questions: what degraded the volume - hardware, noisy neighbor?
action_items: add io_wait alerting ahead of query latency
